title: Roberta Cowell transitions and amends her birth certificate
description: Roberta Cowell, a former racing driver and RAF pilot, is recognised as the first known British trans woman to undergo gender-confirmation surgery and to have the sex on her birth certificate changed.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Wolfenden Committee convened on homosexual offences
description: A government committee chaired by John Wolfenden was set up to examine the law on homosexual offences and prostitution, prompted by prosecutions of prominent men for indecency.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Wolfenden Report urges decriminalisation of gay sex
description: The Wolfenden Committee published its report recommending that consensual same-sex acts between adults in private should no longer be a criminal offence, laying the groundwork for later reform.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Homosexual Law Reform Society launches
description: Campaigners established the Homosexual Law Reform Society to press for the legalisation of consensual same-sex relations along the lines the Wolfenden Report had proposed.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Minorities Research Group opens first UK lesbian organisation
description: The Minorities Research Group set up Britain's first lesbian social and political organisation and began publishing the journal Arena Three, giving lesbians a national point of contact.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: North Western Homosexual Law Reform Committee forms
description: Activists in the north of England founded the North Western Homosexual Law Reform Committee to campaign regionally for changes to the law on same-sex relations.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Beaumont Society founded to support trans people
description: The Beaumont Society was established as a support group for transvestite and transgender people, becoming one of the earliest such organisations in Britain.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Sexual Offences Act decriminalises gay sex in England and Wales
description: The Sexual Offences Act 1967 partially decriminalised sex between men aged over 21 conducted in private in England and Wales, ten years after the Wolfenden Report recommended reform.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Reform committee becomes the Campaign for Homosexual Equality
description: The North Western Homosexual Law Reform Committee expanded into a UK-wide body and was renamed the Committee for Homosexual Equality, later the Campaign for Homosexual Equality (CHE).

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall riots erupt in New York City
description: Patrons of the Stonewall Inn in Manhattan resisted a police raid, sparking several nights of protest that became a catalyst for the modern LGBTQ+ rights movement worldwide.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay Liberation Front established in London
description: Activists set up the London Gay Liberation Front, importing the confrontational politics of the post-Stonewall American movement into British campaigning.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Corbett v Corbett fixes legal sex at birth
description: The Corbett v Corbett judgment ruled that a person's legal sex could not be changed from that assigned at birth, setting a precedent that denied trans people legal recognition for decades.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: CHE adopts the name Campaign for Homosexual Equality
description: The Committee for Homosexual Equality changed its name to the Campaign for Homosexual Equality, reflecting a shift toward broader activism.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Nullity of Marriage Act bars same-sex marriage
description: The Nullity of Marriage Act 1971 explicitly defined marriage as between a man and a woman, statutorily prohibiting same-sex couples from marrying in England and Wales.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First London Pride march takes place
description: Britain's first Pride march was held in London and drew around 2,000 participants, establishing the annual event as a fixture of the UK movement.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay News launches as Britain's first gay newspaper
description: Gay News began publication as the first newspaper aimed at a gay readership in Britain, providing news and community coverage.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First British gay rights conference held in Morecambe
description: The Campaign for Homosexual Equality convened the first British gay rights conference in Morecambe, Lancashire, bringing activists together nationally.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Brighton holds its first Pride
description: Brighton staged its first Pride event, beginning what would become one of the largest annual LGBTQ+ celebrations in the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Maureen Colquhoun comes out as first lesbian MP
description: Labour politician Maureen Colquhoun came out as a lesbian, becoming the first Member of Parliament to be publicly known as a lesbian.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: London Lesbian and Gay Switchboard opens
description: The London Lesbian and Gay Switchboard was set up as a telephone helpline offering information and support to LGBTQ+ callers.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stephen Whittle co-founds Manchester trans group
description: Trans activist Stephen Whittle helped establish a transvestite and transsexual support group in Manchester, an early node in what became UK trans organising.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First National trans conference held in Leeds
description: The first National transvestite and transsexual conference took place in Leeds, an early gathering of the UK trans community.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Jan Morris publishes Conundrum
description: Writer Jan Morris released Conundrum, a widely read memoir about her gender transition that brought trans experience to a mainstream audience.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay Left journal begins publication
description: The socialist journal Gay Left started publishing, offering theoretical and political analysis from a gay left perspective.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Liberal Party backs full LGBT equality
description: The Liberal Party became the first UK political party to adopt a policy supporting full equality for lesbian, gay, bi and trans people.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay Christian Movement founded
description: The Gay Christian Movement was established to support LGBTQ+ Christians and to challenge anti-gay attitudes within the churches.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First gay and lesbian TUC conference held
description: The trade union movement held its first gay and lesbian conference, linking workplace organising with LGBTQ+ rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: House of Lords defeats age of consent reform
description: A bill to lower the age of consent for sex between men to 18 was defeated in the House of Lords, leaving the unequal threshold in place.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay News prosecuted for blasphemous libel
description: Gay News faced a private prosecution for blasphemous libel over a poem it published, the first such case in Britain for more than fifty years.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Harry Benjamin gender dysphoria association founded
description: The Harry Benjamin International Gender Dysphoria Association was established, becoming the leading professional body setting standards of care for trans health, later renamed WPATH.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Scotland decriminalises sex between men
description: Legislation decriminalised consensual sex between men aged over 21 in Scotland, extending reform that England and Wales had seen in 1967.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: BBC airs A Change of Sex documentary
description: The BBC broadcast A Change of Sex, following a trans woman's transition and bringing trans lives to a national television audience.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First Black gay and lesbian group formed
description: Britain's first Black gay and lesbian group was established, creating a space for LGBTQ+ people of colour within the wider movement.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: European court finds Northern Ireland ban unlawful
description: In the Dudgeon case a court found that criminalising same-sex acts in Northern Ireland breached the European Convention on Human Rights, forcing legal change.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First UK AIDS case recorded
description: The first UK case of AIDS was recorded at Brompton Hospital in London, marking the start of an epidemic that would profoundly affect gay and bi men.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: London Bisexual Group formed
description: The London Bisexual Group was established, one of the earliest organised bisexual communities in the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Northern Ireland decriminalises sex between men
description: The Homosexual Offences (Northern Ireland) Order decriminalised consensual sex between men in Northern Ireland, following the Dudgeon ruling.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Terry Higgins dies of an AIDS-related illness
description: Terry Higgins became one of the first named people in the UK to die of an AIDS-related illness, an event that galvanised community responses to the epidemic.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Terrence Higgins Trust established
description: Friends of Terry Higgins set up a charity in his name, later the Terrence Higgins Trust, which became Britain's leading HIV and sexual health organisation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Guernsey decriminalises sex between men
description: Guernsey decriminalised consensual sex between men, extending law reform to the Channel Islands.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gay and bi men asked not to give blood
description: Men who have sex with men were asked not to donate blood as fears about HIV transmission grew, the start of long-running restrictions on donation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Channel 4 airs first UK lesbian and gay TV show
description: One in Five became the first national lesbian and gay television programme in the UK, broadcast on Channel 4.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Chris Smith becomes first openly gay MP
description: Labour politician Chris Smith came out, becoming the first sitting Member of Parliament to be openly gay.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First national bisexual conference launches
description: BiCon, the UK's national bisexual conference, was held for the first time and became a recurring gathering for the bisexual community.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Lesbians and Gays Support the Miners launches
description: The Lesbians and Gays Support the Miners campaign was formed to raise funds for striking miners, forging a notable alliance between LGBTQ+ activists and the labour movement.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Edinburgh Bisexual Group founded
description: The Edinburgh Bisexual Group was established, expanding organised bisexual community in Scotland.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Mark Rees takes trans rights case to European court
description: Mark Rees brought a case to the European Court of Human Rights challenging the UK's refusal to legally recognise his gender, an early test of trans rights in Strasbourg.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: International Foundation for Gender Education founded
description: The International Foundation for Gender Education was established to advocate for and provide resources to the trans community.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Section 28 bans promotion of homosexuality
description: The government introduced Section 28 of the Local Government Act 1988, prohibiting local authorities and schools from what it called promoting homosexuality, a measure that stigmatised LGBTQ+ people for years.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Ian McKellen comes out in response to Section 28
description: Actor Ian McKellen came out publicly on BBC radio while opposing Section 28, becoming a prominent voice for LGBTQ+ rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Denmark recognises first same-sex partnerships
description: Denmark became the first country in the world to grant legal recognition to same-sex couples through registered partnerships. Stonewall's timeline dates this to 1988, though the law took effect in 1989.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall UK founded to oppose Section 28
description: Campaigners established Stonewall as a professional lobbying organisation in response to Section 28, going on to become the UK's leading LGBTQ+ rights charity.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Out on Tuesday LGBT series airs on Channel 4
description: Channel 4 broadcast Out on Tuesday, a magazine series made by and for LGBTQ+ viewers.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: OutRage! direct action group formed
description: The direct action group OutRage! was founded to campaign confrontationally against homophobia and for LGBTQ+ rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Lesbian and Gay Police Association established
description: The Lesbian and Gay Police Association was set up to support LGBTQ+ officers and staff within UK policing.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Manchester holds its first Pride
description: Manchester staged its first Pride event, which grew into one of the UK's largest annual LGBTQ+ celebrations.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Northern Ireland holds its first Pride parade
description: Northern Ireland held its first Pride parade, establishing a visible LGBTQ+ presence in Belfast.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Jersey decriminalises sex between men
description: Jersey decriminalised consensual sex between men, continuing law reform across the Channel Islands.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Justin Fashanu comes out as gay footballer
description: Justin Fashanu became the first professional footballer in Britain to come out publicly as gay, a rare act in the sport at the time.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Fringe meeting sows the seed for Press for Change
description: A Liberal Democrat conference fringe meeting on trans issues led to organising that would establish the trans lobbying group Press for Change.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: WHO declassifies homosexuality as mental illness
description: The World Health Organisation removed same-sex attraction from its list of mental disorders, a globally significant step in depathologising homosexuality.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Isle of Man repeals its sodomy law
description: The Isle of Man repealed legislation criminalising sex between men, an early step toward decriminalisation on the island.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Press for Change established as trans lobby
description: Press for Change was formally launched as a lobbying and legal campaigning organisation working for trans people's rights in the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Age of consent for gay sex lowered to 18
description: The House of Commons voted to equalise the age of consent at 16, but the House of Lords rejected it and the threshold for sex between men was instead lowered to 18.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Isle of Man fully decriminalises homosexuality
description: The Isle of Man completed the decriminalisation of consensual same-sex relations on the island.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Sandi Toksvig comes out as lesbian
description: Broadcaster Sandi Toksvig came out publicly as a lesbian, an early high-profile coming out in British public life.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Mermaids founded to support trans youth
description: Parents of gender-variant children founded Mermaids to support trans and gender-diverse young people and their families.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall campaigns against military service ban
description: Stonewall launched a campaign to end the ban on lesbian, gay and bi people serving in the armed forces.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: European court protects a trans employee from bias
description: In P v S and Cornwall County Council the European Court of Justice ruled that dismissing an employee for being trans was unlawful sex discrimination, a landmark for trans workplace rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: The Archers introduces its first gay character
description: The radio soap The Archers introduced Sean Myerson as its first openly gay character, a notable moment for LGBTQ+ representation in mainstream British broadcasting.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Twigg and Bradshaw elected as openly gay MPs
description: At the general election Stephen Twigg and, minutes later, Ben Bradshaw were elected as openly gay Members of Parliament.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: UK recognises same-sex partners for immigration
description: The UK government began recognising same-sex partners for immigration purposes, allowing couples to be treated as a unit under the rules.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Angela Eagle comes out as first lesbian MP to do so
description: Labour MP Angela Eagle came out, becoming the first Member of Parliament to voluntarily come out as a lesbian while in office.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Equality Network formed in Scotland
description: The Equality Network was established in Scotland to campaign for LGBTQ+ rights and to inform Scottish policy and law.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Euan Sutherland challenges unequal age of consent
description: Euan Sutherland lodged a complaint with the European Commission of Human Rights arguing that the unequal age of consent for sex between men breached his rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Lords again blocks equal age of consent
description: The House of Commons voted to lower the age of consent for same-sex relations to 16, but the House of Lords defeated the clause a month later.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Waheed Alli becomes first openly gay peer
description: Waheed Alli took his seat as the first openly gay member of the House of Lords.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Bolton 7 convicted of gross indecency
description: A group of men known as the Bolton 7 were convicted of gross indecency over consensual acts, and the case was taken to the European Court of Human Rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Transgender Day of Remembrance founded
description: Trans Day of Remembrance was founded in the United States to honour trans people killed in acts of violence, later observed internationally each November.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Admiral Duncan pub bombed in Soho
description: A nail bomb planted by David Copeland exploded at the Admiral Duncan, a gay pub in Soho, killing three people and injuring around seventy in a targeted attack.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Michael Cashman becomes first openly gay UK MEP
description: Michael Cashman was elected as the first openly gay UK member of the European Parliament.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: European court rules against military discharge
description: The European Court of Human Rights found that discharging personnel from the Royal Navy for being gay breached their right to private life under Article 8.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Bi Visibility Day founded on 23 September
description: Activists founded Bi Visibility Day, marked each 23 September to raise awareness of bisexual people and challenge biphobia.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

skip_reason: unverifiable — the page's 'March 29, 2000 first same-sex marriages in England and Wales' entry appears to be an extraction/date error; same-sex marriage in England and Wales did not begin until 2014 (see ref 108).

title: Ban on LGB people in the armed forces lifted
description: The UK lifted its ban on lesbian, gay and bi people serving in the armed forces, following the European court ruling.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Scotland abolishes Section 28
description: The Scottish Parliament repealed Section 28, becoming the first part of the UK to remove the clause restricting discussion of homosexuality in schools.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Age of consent equalised at 16
description: The Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act 2000 lowered the age of consent for sex between men to 16, finally matching that for opposite-sex couples, and decriminalised group sex between men.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall launches Diversity Champions programme
description: Stonewall established its Diversity Champions programme to help employers create LGBTQ+-inclusive workplaces.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Equal age of consent takes effect across the UK
description: The equalised age of consent of 16 came into force across the whole of the UK, ending the last legal distinction on the age of consent.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Same-sex couples gain equal adoption rights
description: The Adoption and Children Act 2002 granted same-sex couples the right to adopt jointly in England and Wales.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Brian Dowling becomes first openly gay kids' TV host
description: Brian Dowling became the first openly gay presenter of a British children's television programme.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Alan Duncan first Conservative MP out by choice
description: Alan Duncan became the first Conservative Member of Parliament to voluntarily come out as gay.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: European court secures trans birth certificates
description: In Goodwin v the United Kingdom the European Court of Human Rights ruled that the UK must legally recognise trans people and issue new birth certificates, driving the Gender Recognition Act.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Section 28 repealed in England, Wales and Northern Ireland
description: Section 28 was repealed across England, Wales and Northern Ireland, removing the restriction on the discussion of homosexuality in schools.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Regulations ban anti-gay workplace discrimination
description: The Employment Equality (Sexual Orientation) Regulations came into effect, prohibiting discrimination against workers on the grounds of sexual orientation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall Cymru launches in Wales
description: Stonewall Cymru was established to campaign for LGBTQ+ equality specifically in Wales.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Kitzinger and Wilkinson marry abroad, unrecognised at home
description: Celia Kitzinger and Sue Wilkinson legally married in Canada, but UK law refused to recognise the marriage, prompting a legal challenge.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Civil Partnership Act passed
description: Parliament passed the Civil Partnership Act 2004, creating a legal status for same-sex couples with most of the rights and responsibilities of marriage.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gender Recognition Act passed
description: Parliament passed the Gender Recognition Act 2004, allowing trans people to gain full legal recognition of their acquired gender for the first time.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall launches its workplace equality index
description: Stonewall introduced the Corporate Equality Index, later the Workplace Equality Index, benchmarking employers on LGBTQ+ inclusion.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Courts gain power over homophobic hate crime
description: Section 146 of the Criminal Justice Act 2003 came into force, empowering courts to impose tougher sentences for crimes aggravated by hostility to sexual orientation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First UK civil partnership registered
description: Christopher Cramp and Matthew Roche entered into the first civil partnership in the UK after the Civil Partnership Act came into force.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: European court rules on trans pension rights
description: In Grant v the United Kingdom the European Court of Human Rights ruled on the pension rights of a trans woman, reinforcing legal recognition of acquired gender.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Isle of Man equalises consent and drops Section 28
description: The Isle of Man equalised the age of consent and repealed its version of Section 28, bringing island law closer to UK standards.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Regulations outlaw discrimination in goods and services
description: The Equality Act (Sexual Orientation) Regulations 2007 outlawed discrimination on grounds of sexual orientation in the provision of goods, facilities and services.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Scotland grants same-sex adoption and fostering equality
description: Scotland passed legislation giving same-sex couples equal rights to adopt and foster children.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First openly LGBT members elected to Scottish Parliament
description: Several openly LGBT politicians were elected as Members of the Scottish Parliament, increasing LGBTQ+ representation in Scotland's legislature.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Same-sex couples recognised as legal parents
description: The Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 2008 allowed same-sex couples to be recognised as the legal parents of children conceived through assisted reproduction.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gendered Intelligence founded
description: Gendered Intelligence was established to support trans and gender-diverse people, especially young people, and to improve understanding of gender diversity.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall studies lesbian and bi women's health
description: Stonewall carried out the first large-scale study of the health of lesbian and bisexual women in the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: David Cameron apologises for Section 28
description: Conservative leader David Cameron publicly apologised for his party's introduction of Section 28.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Equality Act protects gender reassignment
description: The Equality Act 2010 consolidated anti-discrimination law and made gender reassignment a protected characteristic alongside sexual orientation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Armed forces join Diversity Champions programme
description: The UK armed forces joined Stonewall's Diversity Champions programme, signalling a shift toward LGBTQ+ inclusion in the military.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: New offence targets incitement to homophobic hatred
description: A new offence of incitement to homophobic hatred came into force, extending protection against hate speech to sexual orientation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Lifetime blood donation ban replaced with deferral
description: The lifetime ban on blood donation by gay and bi men was replaced with a 12-month deferral period after last sexual contact.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Civil partnerships allowed in religious buildings
description: An amendment permitted civil partnership ceremonies to take place in religious buildings where congregations chose to host them.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Ruth Davidson becomes first out party leader
description: Ruth Davidson was elected leader of the Scottish Conservatives, becoming the first openly gay leader of a UK political party.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Court backs gay couple in B&B discrimination case
description: UK courts ruled in favour of Martin Hall and Steven Preddy after a bed and breakfast refused them a double room, upholding protection against discrimination in services.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Historic convictions can be removed under new law
description: The Protection of Freedoms Act allowed men to apply to have historic convictions for consensual same-sex acts, no longer offences, removed from their records.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Ofsted framework names homophobic bullying
description: The Ofsted school inspection framework was updated to include explicit reference to homophobic bullying, prompting schools to address it.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Same-sex marriage passes for England and Wales
description: Parliament passed the Marriage (Same-Sex Couples) Act, legalising same-sex marriage in England and Wales.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Alan Turing granted posthumous royal pardon
description: Codebreaker Alan Turing, convicted of gross indecency in 1952, received a posthumous royal pardon, later a model for wider pardons of gay and bi men.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First Trans Pride held in Brighton
description: Brighton hosted the first Trans Pride event in the UK, creating a dedicated celebration of the trans community.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First same-sex marriages take place in England and Wales
description: The first same-sex marriages were held in England and Wales under the Marriage (Same-Sex Couples) Act 2013.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Scotland passes same-sex marriage legislation
description: The Scottish Government passed legislation legalising same-sex marriage in Scotland.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall extends its remit to trans equality
description: Stonewall broadened its mission to campaign explicitly for trans equality alongside lesbian, gay and bi rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Royal Vauxhall Tavern listed for LGBT history
description: The Royal Vauxhall Tavern in London became the first UK building granted listed status specifically on the basis of its LGBT history.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: David Mundell first out gay Conservative cabinet minister
description: David Mundell came out as gay, becoming the first openly gay Conservative cabinet minister.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Isle of Man legalises same-sex marriage
description: The Isle of Man legalised same-sex marriage, extending marriage equality to the island.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gunman kills 49 at Pulse nightclub in Orlando
description: A gunman opened fire at the Pulse LGBTQ+ nightclub in Orlando, killing 49 people and injuring 53, one of the deadliest attacks on the community; vigils were held worldwide.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Justine Greening first out female cabinet member
description: Justine Greening announced she was in a same-sex relationship on the day of Pride, becoming the first openly LGBT female cabinet member.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Stonewall Inn declared a US national monument
description: President Obama designated the Stonewall Inn and surrounding area as America's first national monument dedicated to LGBTQ+ history.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: UK pardons men convicted under historic laws
description: The UK government issued a posthumous pardon, and a route to pardons for the living, to gay and bi men convicted under historic sexual offences laws, known as the Turing law.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Relationships education made mandatory in schools
description: Amendments to the Children and Social Work Bill made relationships and sex education compulsory in schools from 2019, requiring LGBTQ+-inclusive content.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Supreme Court orders end to same-sex pension gap
description: The UK Supreme Court ruled that discrimination against same-sex couples in survivor pension entitlements must end immediately.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Blood donation deferral cut to three months
description: The Department of Health reduced the blood donation deferral period for gay and bi men from twelve months to three.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Scotland pardons pre-decriminalisation convictions
description: The Scottish Government pardoned gay and bi men convicted before the 1981 decriminalisation of same-sex acts in Scotland.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: UK announces plans to ban conversion therapy
description: The UK government announced proposals to ban conversion therapy aimed at changing sexual orientation or gender identity.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Gender Recognition Act consultation draws mass response
description: A public consultation on reforming the Gender Recognition Act drew more than 100,000 responses, reflecting intense debate over trans legal recognition.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Fiftieth anniversary of the Stonewall uprising marked
description: The fiftieth anniversary of the 1969 Stonewall uprising was widely commemorated, including in the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: New curriculum requires LGBT rights teaching in England
description: A new PSHE curriculum was introduced in England requiring schools to acknowledge LGBT people and rights.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: First Bi Pride UK event held
description: The first Bi Pride UK event took place, giving the bisexual community a dedicated national celebration.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Same-sex marriage legalised in Northern Ireland
description: Same-sex marriage became legal in Northern Ireland, completing marriage equality across the UK.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Layla Moran first out pansexual UK parliamentarian
description: Layla Moran came out as pansexual, becoming the first openly pansexual member of the UK Parliament.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Blood donation rules to focus on individual risk
description: The government announced that blood donation rules would move to an individualised risk assessment rather than blanket restrictions on gay and bi men.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: UK census asks about gender identity and orientation
description: For the first time the UK census included voluntary questions on gender identity and sexual orientation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Government announces plan to ban conversion practices
description: The government set out plans to ban conversion practices and to establish a support fund for survivors.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Etherton review documents armed forces abuse
description: Lord Etherton published a review documenting the abuse and harassment of LGBTQ+ personnel in the British Armed Forces under the former ban.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: UK blocks Scottish gender recognition reform
description: The UK government blocked the Gender Recognition Reform (Scotland) Bill from receiving royal assent, the first use of such a veto over Scottish legislation.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Puberty blockers withdrawn for under-18s
description: The UK government stopped the routine prescription of puberty blockers to people under 18 following the Cass Review of gender identity services.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Permanent ban on puberty-suppressing hormones imposed
description: The UK government imposed a permanent ban on the sale and supply of puberty-suppressing hormones for gender-related treatment of minors.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: LGBTQ+ veterans begin receiving compensation
description: LGBTQ+ veterans affected by the former ban on serving in the armed forces began receiving financial compensation following the Etherton Review.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality

title: Supreme Court defines sex as biological for Equality Act
description: The UK Supreme Court ruled that the terms sex, woman and man in the Equality Act 2010 refer to biological sex assigned at birth, a decision with significant implications for trans people.

sources.label: Stonewall UK — Key dates for LGBTQ+ equality
